perturbation.original_text: For the purposes of this Non-Disclosure Agreement, the term "Confidential Information" shall mean material, information, data and other communications (a) disclosed by NYNEX and/or one or more of its parent, subsidiary or affiliated corporations, appropriately marked as "Confidential," "Proprietary" or the like or otherwise disclosed in a manner consistent with its proprietary and confidential nature; or (b) produced during the course of the working relationship between NYNEX and CONSULTANT, which would either give NYNEX' competitors an advantage or diminish or eliminate NYNEX' advantage over its competitors.
perturbation.changed_text: For the purposes of this Non-Disclosure Agreement, the term "Protected Data" shall mean material, information, data and other communications (a) disclosed by NYNEX and/or one or more of its parent, subsidiary or affiliated corporations, appropriately marked as "Confidential," "Proprietary" or the like or otherwise disclosed in a manner consistent with its proprietary and confidential nature; or (b) produced during the course of the working relationship between NYNEX and CONTRACTOR, which would either give NYNEX' competitors an advantage or diminish or eliminate NYNEX' advantage over its competitors.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Protected Data' and 'CONSULTANT' to 'CONTRACTOR' in the definition, while subsequent clauses still refer to 'Confidential Information' creates confusion as to which data is protected under the agreement and also confusion on who the contract applies too. This leads to an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ALL NYNEX Confidential Information: (a) is hereby acknowledged by CONSULTANT to be of a proprietary nature to, and to constitute secrets of NYNEX; (b) shall not be copied, used, distributed, disclosed, disseminated or communicated in any way or form by CONSULTANT whether or not for its own benefit, to anyone outside or within its own organization, except on a "need-to-know" basis to the extent necessary for: (i) negotiations, discussions and consultations with personnel or authorized representatives of NYNEX; (ii) supplying NYNEX with products or services at its order; (iii) preparing bids, estimates and proposals for submission to NYNEX; and (iv) any other purpose which NYNEX may authorize in writing; (c) shall not be used by CONSULTANT for any purposes other than those set forth herein, without the experts prior written permission of NYNEX; (d) shall be held by CONSULTANT in the strictest confidence, and shall be treated by it with the same degree of care to avoid disclosure to any third party as is used with respect to CONSULTANT'S own information of like importance, or, a minimum, shall be treated with a reasonable degree of care to avoid any such disclosure. CONSULTANT shall be liable for the disclosure of Confidential Information of <PAGE> NYNEX if such care is not used. The burden shall be upon CONSULTANT to show that such care, in fact, was used; and (e) Confidential Information is hereby acknowledged by CONSULTANT to be the sole property of NYNEX and shall be returned to NYNEX (including, without limitation, all materials, documents, drawings, models, apparatus, sketches, designs, specifications and lists, encompassing or evidencing same or related thereto, and all copies/formats thereof), within seven (7) days after receipt by CONSULTANT of a written request from NYNEX setting forth the Confidential Information to be returned. Upon receipt of such request, CONSULTANT also shall erase or destroy any such Confidential Information in any computer memory or data storage apparatus.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Confidential Information' and 'CONSULTANT'. The changed text redefines these as 'Protected Data' and 'CONTRACTOR' respectively. However, the 'Contradicted Text' still refers to 'NYNEX Confidential Information' and 'CONSULTANT'. This creates a direct contradiction because the changed definition means the 'Contradicted Text' is referring to a term that is no longer defined, creating ambiguity and unenforceability. The contract is no longer consistent on which data is protected and who the contract applies to, leading to a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: ALL NYNEX Confidential Information: (a) is hereby acknowledged by CONSULTANT to be of a proprietary nature to, and to constitute secrets of NYNEX; (b) shall not be copied, used, distributed, disclosed, disseminated or communicated in any way or form by CONSULTANT whether or not for its own benefit, to anyone outside or within its own organization, except on a "need-to-know" basis to the extent necessary for: (i) negotiations, discussions and consultations with personnel or authorized representatives of NYNEX; (ii) supplying NYNEX with products or services at its order; (iii) preparing bids, estimates and proposals for submission to NYNEX; and (iv) any other purpose which NYNEX may authorize in writing; (c) shall not be used by CONSULTANT for any purposes other than those set forth herein, without the experts prior written permission of NYNEX; (d) shall be held by CONSULTANT in the strictest confidence, and shall be treated by it with the same degree of care to avoid disclosure to any third party as is used with respect to CONSULTANT'S own information of like importance, or, a minimum, shall be treated with a reasonable degree of care to avoid any such disclosure. CONSULTANT shall be liable for the disclosure of Confidential Information of <PAGE> NYNEX if such care is not used. The burden shall be upon CONSULTANT to show that such care, in fact, was used; and (e) Confidential Information is hereby acknowledged by CONSULTANT to be the sole property of NYNEX and shall be returned to NYNEX (including, without limitation, all materials, documents, drawings, models, apparatus, sketches, designs, specifications and lists, encompassing or evidencing same or related thereto, and all copies/formats thereof), within seven (7) days after receipt by CONSULTANT of a written request from NYNEX setting forth the Confidential Information to be returned. Upon receipt of such request, CONSULTANT also shall erase or destroy any such Confidential Information in any computer memory or data storage apparatus.
perturbation.changed_text: ALL NYNEX Sensitive Information: (a) is hereby acknowledged by CONTRACTOR to be of a proprietary nature to, and to constitute secrets of NYNEX; (b) shall not be copied, used, distributed, disclosed, disseminated or communicated in any way or form by CONTRACTOR whether or not for its own benefit, to anyone outside or within its own organization, except on a "need-to-know" basis to the extent necessary for: (i) negotiations, discussions and consultations with personnel or authorized representatives of NYNEX; (ii) supplying NYNEX with products or services at its order; (iii) preparing bids, estimates and proposals for submission to NYNEX; and (iv) any other purpose which NYNEX may authorize in writing; (c) shall not be used by CONTRACTOR for any purposes other than those set forth herein, without the experts prior written permission of NYNEX; (d) shall be held by CONTRACTOR in the strictest confidence, and shall be treated by it with the same degree of care to avoid disclosure to any third party as is used with respect to CONTRACTOR'S own information of like importance, or, a minimum, shall be treated with a reasonable degree of care to avoid any such disclosure. CONTRACTOR shall be liable for the disclosure of Sensitive Information of <PAGE> NYNEX if such care is not used. The burden shall be upon CONTRACTOR to show that such care, in fact, was used; and (e) Sensitive Information is hereby acknowledged by CONTRACTOR to be the sole property of NYNEX and shall be returned to NYNEX (including, without limitation, all materials, documents, drawings, models, apparatus, sketches, designs, specifications and lists, encompassing or evidencing same or related thereto, and all copies/formats thereof), within seven (7) days after receipt by CONTRACTOR of a written request from NYNEX setting forth the Sensitive Information to be returned. Upon receipt of such request, CONTRACTOR also shall erase or destroy any such Sensitive Information in any computer memory or data storage apparatus.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Sensitive Information' and 'CONSULTANT' to 'CONTRACTOR' in this section, while other sections still use the original terms, creates confusion about which information and which party this paragraph applies to, leading to contradictory obligations within the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The obligations set forth in Paragraph 2 hereof shall not apply, or shall terminate, with respect to any particular portion of NYNEX Confidential Information which: (a) was in CONSULTANT's possession, free of any obligation of confidence, prior to receipt from NYNEX, as proven by CONSULTANT's written records; provided, however, that CONSULTANT immediately informs NYNEX, in writing, to establish its prior possession; (b) is already in the public domain at the time NYNEX communicates it to CONSULTANT, or becomes available to the public through no breach of this Non-Disclosure Agreement by CONSULTANT; (c) is received independently from a third party free to disclose such information to CONSULTANT; (d) is developed by CONSULTANT, independently of and without reference to any Confidential Information of NYNEX or any other information that NYNEX has disclosed in confidence to any third party, as proven by CONSULTANT's written records; (e) is disclosed by CONSULTANT to a third party, with the express prior written permission of NYNEX; (f) is disclosed by CONSULTANT in order to satisfy any legal requirement of any competent government body; provided, however, that immediately upon CONSULTANT's receipt of any such request, CONSULTANT shall first advise NYNEX of same before making any disclosure to such body, so that NYNEX may either interpose an objection to such disclosure before such body, or take action to assure confidential handling of the Confidential Information by such body, or take other action to protect the Confidential Information which NYNEX deems appropriate under the circumstances; or (g) in any event, five (5) years after the date of execution of this Non-Disclosure Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Sensitive Information' and 'CONSULTANT' with 'CONTRACTOR'. The contradicted text explicitly refers to 'NYNEX Confidential Information' and 'CONSULTANT'. This creates a contradiction because the contradicted text's obligations and exceptions now apply to a term that may or may not be the same as the one defined in the changed text. This ambiguity makes it unclear which information and party the original obligations still apply to, leading to a direct conflict and potential unenforceability. The term is misaligned causing a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The obligations set forth in Paragraph 2 hereof shall not apply, or shall terminate, with respect to any particular portion of NYNEX Confidential Information which: (a) was in CONSULTANT's possession, free of any obligation of confidence, prior to receipt from NYNEX, as proven by CONSULTANT's written records; provided, however, that CONSULTANT immediately informs NYNEX, in writing, to establish its prior possession; (b) is already in the public domain at the time NYNEX communicates it to CONSULTANT, or becomes available to the public through no breach of this Non-Disclosure Agreement by CONSULTANT; (c) is received independently from a third party free to disclose such information to CONSULTANT; (d) is developed by CONSULTANT, independently of and without reference to any Confidential Information of NYNEX or any other information that NYNEX has disclosed in confidence to any third party, as proven by CONSULTANT's written records; (e) is disclosed by CONSULTANT to a third party, with the express prior written permission of NYNEX; (f) is disclosed by CONSULTANT in order to satisfy any legal requirement of any competent government body; provided, however, that immediately upon CONSULTANT's receipt of any such request, CONSULTANT shall first advise NYNEX of same before making any disclosure to such body, so that NYNEX may either interpose an objection to such disclosure before such body, or take action to assure confidential handling of the Confidential Information by such body, or take other action to protect the Confidential Information which NYNEX deems appropriate under the circumstances; or (g) in any event, five (5) years after the date of execution of this Non-Disclosure Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations set forth in Paragraph 2 hereof shall not apply, or shall terminate, with respect to any particular portion of NYNEX Sensitive Data which: (a) was in CONTRACTOR's possession, free of any obligation of confidence, prior to receipt from NYNEX, as proven by CONTRACTOR's written records; provided, however, that CONTRACTOR immediately informs NYNEX, in writing, to establish its prior possession; (b) is already in the public domain at the time NYNEX communicates it to CONTRACTOR, or becomes available to the public through no breach of this Non-Disclosure Agreement by CONTRACTOR; (c) is received independently from a third party free to disclose such information to CONTRACTOR; (d) is developed by CONTRACTOR, independently of and without reference to any Sensitive Data of NYNEX or any other information that NYNEX has disclosed in confidence to any third party, as proven by CONTRACTOR's written records; (e) is disclosed by CONTRACTOR to a third party, with the express prior written permission of NYNEX; (f) is disclosed by CONTRACTOR in order to satisfy any legal requirement of any competent government body; provided, however, that immediately upon CONTRACTOR's receipt of any such request, CONTRACTOR shall first advise NYNEX of same before making any disclosure to such body, so that NYNEX may either interpose an objection to such disclosure before such body, or take action to assure confidential handling of the Sensitive Data by such body, or take other action to protect the Sensitive Data which NYNEX deems appropriate under the circumstances; or (g) in any event, five (5) years after the date of execution of this Non-Disclosure Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Sensitive Data' and 'CONSULTANT' to 'CONTRACTOR', while other sections still use the original terms 'Confidential Information' and 'CONSULTANT', creates a contradiction. It's unclear which set of terms applies, especially when referencing paragraph 2, which now has different definitions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: CONSULTANT warrants and represents that CONSULTANT has bound its employees, agents and subcontractors to the terms and conditions of this Agreement or that each and every employee, agent or subcontractor has personally executed a Non-Disclosure Agreement containing terms and conditions no less stringent than the terms and conditions contained herein; and furthermore CONSULTANT hereby <PAGE> agrees to indemnify, hold harmless and defend NYNEX from and against any loss, cost, damage, expense or claim arising out of any breach of this provision or the failure of CONSULTANT or its employees, agents or subcontractors to protect such Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Confidential Information' and 'CONSULTANT', while the changed text replaces these with 'Sensitive Data' and 'CONTRACTOR'. The 'Contradicted Text' still uses the original terms, 'CONSULTANT' and 'Confidential Information'. Because the modified clause redefines these terms, but the contradicted text still uses old terms, this creates a conflict about which set of terms applies. This is a Misaligned Terminology contradiction as defined, thus the answer is YES.

perturbation.original_text: CONSULTANT warrants and represents that CONSULTANT has bound its employees, agents and subcontractors to the terms and conditions of this Agreement or that each and every employee, agent or subcontractor has personally executed a Non-Disclosure Agreement containing terms and conditions no less stringent than the terms and conditions contained herein; and furthermore CONSULTANT hereby <PAGE> agrees to indemnify, hold harmless and defend NYNEX from and against any loss, cost, damage, expense or claim arising out of any breach of this provision or the failure of CONSULTANT or its employees, agents or subcontractors to protect such Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: CONTRACTOR warrants and represents that CONTRACTOR has bound its employees, agents and subcontractors to the terms and conditions of this Agreement or that each and every employee, agent or subcontractor has personally executed a Non-Disclosure Agreement containing terms and conditions no less stringent than the terms and conditions contained herein; and furthermore CONTRACTOR hereby <PAGE> agrees to indemnify, hold harmless and defend NYNEX from and against any loss, cost, damage, expense or claim arising out of any breach of this provision or the failure of CONTRACTOR or its employees, agents or subcontractors to protect such Sensitive Data.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'CONSULTANT' to 'CONTRACTOR' and 'Confidential Information' to 'Sensitive Data' in Paragraph 6, when the rest of the agreement refers to the original terminology, creates confusion about who this warranty applies to and what information is being protected. This leads to an in-text contradiction, because the rest of the document still defines and discusses "CONSULTANT" and "Confidential Information".
perturbation.contradicted_text: CONSULTANT further agrees that it shall not, without the prior written consent of NYNEX, make any news release, public announcement, or denial or confirmation of all or any part of the discussions or negotiations, or in any manner advertise or publish the fact that the parties have entered into discussions or negotiations with each other, or disclose any details connected with such discussions or negotiations to any third party, including any disclosure with respect to this Non-Disclosure Agreement, the negotiations culminating herein, or any phase of any program hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'CONSULTANT' and 'Confidential Information'. The changed text replaces these terms with 'CONTRACTOR' and 'Sensitive Data' respectively. The contradicted text refers to 'CONSULTANT' and implies 'Confidential Information' through the context of Non-Disclosure Agreement. The change creates an in-text contradiction because the agreement now uses inconsistent terminology, specifically referencing two different actors (CONSULTANT vs CONTRACTOR) when it should only refer to one, and protecting two different types of information (Confidential Information vs Sensitive Data) without clarifying if they are meant to be interchangeable. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: CONSULTANT further agrees that it shall not, without the prior written consent of NYNEX, make any news release, public announcement, or denial or confirmation of all or any part of the discussions or negotiations, or in any manner advertise or publish the fact that the parties have entered into discussions or negotiations with each other, or disclose any details connected with such discussions or negotiations to any third party, including any disclosure with respect to this Non-Disclosure Agreement, the negotiations culminating herein, or any phase of any program hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: CONTRACTOR further agrees that it shall not, without the prior written consent of NYNEX, make any news release, public announcement, or denial or confirmation of all or any part of the discussions or negotiations, or in any manner advertise or publish the fact that the parties have entered into discussions or negotiations with each other, or disclose any details connected with such discussions or negotiations to any third party, including any disclosure with respect to this Confidentiality Agreement, the negotiations culminating herein, or any phase of any program hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'CONSULTANT' to 'CONTRACTOR' and 'Non-Disclosure Agreement' to 'Confidentiality Agreement' in paragraph 8 introduces further inconsistency. Now, some sections refer to 'CONSULTANT', others to 'CONTRACTOR', and the type of agreement itself is inconsistently named. This in-text contradiction makes the obligations unclear.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No term or provision of this Non-Disclosure Agreement shall be deemed waived, and no breach excused, unless such waiver or consent shall be in writing and signed by the party claimed to have waived or consented. Any consent by any party to, or waiver of, a breach by the other, whether express or implied, shall not constitute a consent to waiver of, or be cause for, any other, different or subsequent breach.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'CONSULTANT' to 'CONTRACTOR' and 'Non-Disclosure Agreement' to 'Confidentiality Agreement' creates a direct contradiction with the contradicted text, which explicitly refers to the 'Non-Disclosure Agreement'. The altered text introduces misaligned terminology, making it unclear which entity and agreement the obligations apply to, thus generating a strong in-text contradiction. This aligns with the Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction definition: 'Misaligned terminology also leads to in-text contradictions when the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.'
